key_themes: Resolution of CMA veterinary market probe providing regulatory certainty with no material impact on guidance
Steady revenue and EBITDA growth in interim results despite soft UK consumer conditions
CEO succession and board refresh following leadership transition announcement
Strong Australia expansion and Main Market listing upgrade
Attractive valuation with analyst Moderate Buy rating and ~40% upside to average price target
summary: CVS Group (vet services provider) delivered resilient H1 FY2026 results with revenue up 5.8% and adjusted EBITDA up 3.9%, trading in line with expectations amid softer UK pet spending but with continued strong growth in Australia.[[1]](https://www.investegate.co.uk/announcement/rns/cvs-group--cvsg/interim-results-/9447633)[[2]](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/cvs-group-plc-fra-4c9-170145482.html) The long-running CMA investigation concluded in March 2026 with manageable remedies (price transparency, fee caps) that were less punitive than feared; the company welcomed the certainty and shares rose ~2% with no change to guidance.[[3]](https://www.hl.co.uk/shares/share-research/cvs-group-announcement-cma-final-decision-as-expected)[[3]](https://www.hl.co.uk/shares/share-research/cvs-group-announcement-cma-final-decision-as-expected) CEO Richard Fairman announced plans to retire after 7 years, triggering a ~2.5% share price dip, alongside board changes, but analysts retain a Moderate Buy stance with an average target of 1,724p versus current levels near 1,210-1,220p.[[4]](https://www.morningstar.com/news/alliance-news/1774860061288528700/cvs-group-ceo-to-step-down-as-uk-vet-pricing-probe-concludes)[[5]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/LON/CVSG/forecast/) Overall investor sentiment is cautiously optimistic as regulatory overhang is removed and the valuation looks compelling.
sources: 10+ news/RNS items (LSE, Yahoo Finance, Reuters, Morningstar, HL), ~5 recent X posts (primarily corporate/news reposts around CEO departure), Analyst consensus from MarketBeat and broker notes
